repo_metadata.name: ray
repo_metadata.description: Ray is an AI compute engine. Ray consists of a core distributed runtime and a set of AI Libraries for accelerating ML workloads.

seo_metadata.title: ray by ray-project - Explain, Architecture & Alternatives
seo_metadata.description: Ray is an AI compute engine. Ray consists of a core distributed runtime and a set of AI Libraries for accelerating ML workloads.

content.what_it_does:
ray is a comprehensive Python-based software project developed and maintained by ray-project. The primary focus of this repository is to provide robust solutions in the domain of: Ray is an AI compute engine. Ray consists of a core distributed runtime and a set of AI Libraries for accelerating ML workloads. It heavily intersects with key technological areas including data-science, deep-learning, deployment, distributed, hyperparameter-optimization. According to the project documentation: .. image:: https://github.com/ray-project/ray/raw/master/doc/source/images/ray_header_logo.png .. image:: https://readthedocs.org/projects/ray/badge/?version=master
    :target: http://docs.ray.io/en/master/?badge=master .. image:: https://img.shields.io/badge/Ray-Join%20Slack-blue
    :target: https://www.ray.io/join-slack
key_features: Built natively with Python for optimal performance and ecosystem compatibility.
Open source architecture allowing for extensive community contributions on GitHub.
Supports and implements modern paradigms such as data-science, deep-learning, deployment.
Provides a systematic approach to solving standard domain-specific challenges.
content.architecture_overview: The ray repository is organized following industry standard Python conventions to ensure maintainability and scalability. The codebase is logically grouped around its core functionalities, offering a main entry point and modular components. It leverages modern software package management systems, explicitly relying on configurations found in pyproject.toml. This indicates a canonical Python environment setup where dependencies and scripts are isolated and managed robustly.
content.how_to_run:
To deploy or interact with ray locally, you should typically follow these standard sequential steps:
1. Clone the repository directly from GitHub: `git clone https://github.com/ray-project/ray.git`
2. Navigate into the freshly cloned directory via your terminal.
3. Carefully inspect the root README.md file for highly specific installation prerequisites or build scripts.
4. Execute the primary run command or entry script to instantiate the service or utilize the library.
use_cases: Implementing production-grade systems focused strictly on data-science.
Serving as a foundational building block for developers working with Python architectures.
Integrating custom workflows that require robust deep-learning capabilities.
